import pandas as pd
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
from PIL import Image
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
%matplotlib inline
model = SentenceTransformer("clip-Vit-B-32")

In [42]:
text_description = ["Two dogs in the snow",
                       "A cat on a table",
"A picture of london at night",
"Taj Mahal View",
"A dog sleeping on a couch",
"Two cats fighting in the garden",
                    "Taj Mahal is made by sahajahan",
                    "Goa city is Famous for enjoing with friends",
                    "Kedarnath is moslt famous temple in India"]
In [43]:
def display_image_and_compare(imge_path,text_description):
  img = Image.open(imge_path) # Corrected typo: 'image_path' to 'imge_path'
  text_emb = model.encode(text_description)
  img_emb = model.encode(img)
  # Assuming embeddings are normalized, calculate cosine similarity
  similarity_score = np.dot(img_emb, text_emb.T)
  #Find the best match
  best_match_index = np.argmax(similarity_score)
  best_match_desc = text_description[best_match_index]
  # print the finl output
  print(f"/nBest Match for the image is L {best_match_desc}with score of{similarity_score.max()}")
  # Display the image
  plt.figure(figsize=(6,6))
  plt.imshow(img)
  plt.axis("off")
  plt.title("Image for similarity comparison",fontsize=14)
  plt.show()
In [49]:
display_image_and_compare("/content/D.jpeg",text_description)
